name: Jazzer (Code Intelligence)
pricing_notes: Apache 2.0 open source; CI Fuzz (commercial cloud fuzzing platform) built on Jazzer

auth.notes: CLI/Maven/Gradle plugin; no auth required for OSS version

performance.notes: Coverage-guided JVM fuzzer; runs indefinitely; first crashes typically found within minutes to hours

agent_readiness.notes: Coverage-guided JVM fuzzer based on libFuzzer. Works with Java, Kotlin, Scala, Groovy. JUnit integration for fuzz tests. Finds security vulnerabilities, crashes, and unexpected exceptions in JVM code. Integrated into Google's OSS-Fuzz. Supports structured fuzzing with FuzzedDataProvider. Maven/Gradle integration.

gotchas: JVM startup overhead limits fuzzing throughput vs native fuzzers
Coverage instrumentation via ASM bytecode modification can miss some branches
Setting up effective corpus requires domain knowledge of target code
Finding exploitable issues requires security expertise to triage
best_when: Java/Kotlin libraries handling untrusted input (parsers, deserializers, protocol handlers)
Security-critical JVM code needing automated vulnerability discovery
OSS-Fuzz integration for continuous JVM library fuzzing
JUnit-based test suites wanting to add fuzz tests alongside unit tests
avoid_when: Non-JVM languages (use Atheris for Python, cargo-fuzz for Rust)
Performance-critical fuzzing where JVM overhead is prohibitive
Teams without security expertise to triage crash findings
summary: Jazzer is a coverage-guided fuzzer for JVM languages (Java, Kotlin, Scala) based on libFuzzer. JUnit integration for fuzz tests alongside unit tests. Maven/Gradle plugins. Integrated with Google's OSS-Fuzz for continuous fuzzing of open source JVM libraries. Best for security-critical Java code handling untrusted input. JVM overhead limits throughput vs native fuzzers.
